options.name: -help
options.description: Prints out the command usage

options.name: -allowed
options.description: Specifies the characters that are allowed in the names. Any characters that are not in the allowed list will be removed in the final name. The minimum specification is 10 characters. To allow all the letters from a to z in capital and lower case letters, you must specify all of them. Use -regexp to allow dash '-' as a character range indicator.

options.name: -append_log
options.description: Appends the report to <out_file> specified with the -log_file option. If you omit this option, <out_file> is overwritten. You can specify this option only if you have specified the -log_file option.

options.name: -change_modules
options.description: Only change names inside these modules or modules of the hinsts ((hInst|vCell|topCell)+, optional)

options.name: -design
options.description: Change the design module name

options.name: -first_restricted
options.description: Specifies the characters that cannot be used in the beginning of the name. Those will be removed, including repeated characters. So -first_restricted {_#%$} will change _$abc to just abc.

options.name: -honor_dont_touch
options.description: Don't rename objects that have the dont_touch attribute set

options.name: -hport
options.description: Change hport names on hinsts (but not the top-level design) and matching connected net names.

options.name: -inst
options.description: Change instance names.

options.name: -last_restricted
options.description: Specifies the characters that cannot be used at the ending of a name. Those will be removed, including repeated chars.

options.name: -local
options.description: Turn off recursive descent, so changes are local to the module or design

options.name: -log_file
options.description: Specifies a log file for the output report that shows the old name and the new name for any name changes.

options.name: -map
options.description: Takes a list of string pairs and maps the first string to the second string in each pair. You need to enclose each pair in braces. To be compatible with RTL Compiler usage, double quotes are optionally allowed around the strings. For example, both -map {{"from" "to"} {"AND" "and"}} and -map {{from to} {AND and}} will result in the same output.

options.name: -max_length
options.description: Enforce maximum name length

options.name: -module
options.description: Change module names except for the design module name

options.name: -name_collision
options.description: Only add -prefix or -suffix values if a name change collides with an existing name.

options.name: -net
options.description: Changes Verilog net names except if it connects to a Verilog port in the same module with the same name.

options.name: -nocase
options.description: Changes names that collide if the case is ignored. For example, if both net1 and Net1 exist, one of them will be changed to resolve the collision by appending a suffix, such as _1 or _2.

options.name: -phys_inst
options.description: Change physical inst names (e.g. filler cells) that do not normally appear in the Verilog output

options.name: -port
options.description: Change the design port names (but not hinsts) and matching connected net names.

options.name: -prefix
options.description: Adds a prefix to the names that are changed.

options.name: -quiet
options.description: Suppress messages about individual objects being renamed

options.name: -random
options.description: All modified names will become random names.

options.name: -random_map_dir
options.description: A map-file for each random name is written inside <out_dir>.

options.name: -regexp
options.description: Allows specifying character ranges with a dash '-' in the -allowed option. This is similar to regexp single character matching usage. For example, to allow all alphanumerical characters, along with _ and $ as legal chars, use: -allowed {a-zA-Z0-9_$}

options.name: -replace_string
options.description: Specifies the replacement string for the reserved words, or -restricted strings. Its default value is '_'.

options.name: -reserved_words
options.description: Specifies a list of words to be avoided (reserved words), like 'begin end'. If a complete name exactly matches one of the reserved words (not just a sub-string of the name), then the name will be changed.

options.name: -restricted
options.description: Specifies a list of strings that cannot be used inside a name. Each string is replaced with the -replace_string string. For example, -restricted {ab bc} will replace all 'ab' and 'bc' strings inside the names.

options.name: -suffix
options.description: Adds a suffix to the names that are changed.

options.name: -system_verilog
options.description: Replaces SystemVerilog reserved words. If a complete name exactly matches one of the reserved words (not just a sub-string of the name), then the name will be changed.

options.name: -verilog
options.description: Replaces Verilog reserved words. If a complete name exactly matches with one of the SystemVerilog reserved words (not only a sub-string of the name), then the name will be changed.

options.name: -vhdl
options.description: Fixes names to meet VHDL identifier requirements. It replaces VHDL reserved words. If a complete name exactly matches one of the VHDL reserved words (not only a sub-string of the name), then the name will be changed. It also implicitly uses first_restricted _0123456789 to remove any leading _ or digit chars, and last_restricted _ to remove any trailing _ chars, so you cannot use vhdl together with first_restricted or last_restricted. Finally, this option will remove any repeated _ chars in the middle of any name, and replace it with a single _ char because VHDL does not allow repeated _ chars.
